steps.instruction: 60 °C for 00:25:00 in dry incubator Use glass slide box

Re-hydrate with HistoClear and Ethanol. Create each as a 50mL conical tube, pouring into glass slide box for each step. Pour slowly and not directly onto slides. HistoClear 00:10:00 00:10:00 X 2 times 100% EtOH 00:05:00 00:05:00 X 2 times 90% EtOH 00:03:00 , 45 mL, pure EtOH with 5 mL, DI water 80% EtOH 00:03:00 40 mL, pure EtOH in 10 mL, DI water 70% EtOH 00:03:00 35 mL, pure EtOH in 15 mL, DI water 50% EtOH 00:03:00 , 25 mL, pure EtOH in 25 mL, DI water
Wash in tap water, 00:05:00 00:05:00 00:05:00 X 3 times
Antigen retrieval . Transfer slides to plastic slide box, now, fill to line with retrieval buffer.
Select paraffin slides for IF staining. Make sure to have secondary-only negative controls.
Select one Antigen Retrieval buffer method from below. (full recipe below) 1 mM EDTA , pH 8.0 Tris-EDTA buffer, pH 9.0 Sodium Citrate , pH 6.0 Antigen Retrieval Reagent

substeps.instruction: Select one Antigen Retrieval buffer method from below. (full recipe below) 1 mM EDTA , pH

steps.instruction: Double wash in PBST/ TBST 00:03:00 00:03:00 00:03:00 X 3 times.

Incubate with secondary antibody diluted in 1x PBS/ blocking buffer at Room temperature for 01:00:00, in dark (min 30') in hydration chamber. Turn lab lights off to apply secondary. Species + Fluorophore Concentration Company/ Product # Amit's Antibody diluted in 3%BSA in TBST Usually secondary is [1:500], with GFP/488 at [1:1000]
Double wash in PBST/TBST 00:03:00 00:03:00 00:03:00 X 3 times.
Incubate with 1x DAPI at Room temperature for 00:15:00 in a hydration chamber. Dilute 1:1000 in ddH2O DAPI located in Dom's -4*C box at 1000X
Double wash in PBST/ TBST 00:03:00 00:03:00 00:03:00 X 3 times.

Add one small drop anti-fade mounting medium 10-15 µL to each section, cover, press with plastic pipette tip to remove bubbles, blot clean with paper towel. Store dark at Room temperature, in dark at least 00:15:00 , then move to 4 °C, DRY slide box for Overnight .

Incubate for primary antibody diluted in blocking buffer Leave Overnight at 4 °C in hydration chamber. Add tap water to wells between slides in chamber to prevent evaporation Species-Antigen Concentration Company/ Product # Amit: Antibody diluted in 3%BSA in TBST
Rinse in PBST/ TBST briefly
Incubate slides with blocking buffer in hydration chamber for 00:30:00 - 01:00:00 at Room temperature Amit's: blocking buffer is 5%BSA in TBST Usually primary antibody is at [1:400]
Wash with PBST/ TBST 00:10:00 00:10:00 X 2 times
Permeabilize nuclear membrane with 0.25% Triton X-100 in PBS 00:05:00 to 00:10:00

Wipe away water, circle section with slide pen , then re-add 200uL/slide of tap water. Transfer slides to black hydration box. Cut P-200 pipette tip to prevent high pressure for subsequent steps Use 200uL for all future steps unless noted, aspirating with vacuum
Rinse with tap water, 00:05:00 00:05:00 X 2 times
Move slides to sit at Room temperature (in plastic box + retrieval buffer) Allow to cool to Room temperature, slowly This can take up to 02:00:00 . (Minimum 00:30:00 ), until box warm/cool to touch.

Add DI-water to pressure cooker up to metal plate. (Slides in plastic box with retrieval buffer) Presser cooker on porridge option, which is 00:20:00 Remove after 00:45:00 . Display will read "LO -25" --> which means 25m after porridge completes
1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0 EDTA 0.37 gDistilled water 1 LAdjust to pH 8.0 with NaOHStore at room temperature for 3 months

substeps.instruction: with NaOHStore at room temperature for 3 months

steps.instruction: Tris-EDTA buffer (10 mM Tris base, 1 mM EDTA solution, 0.05% Tween 20, pH 9.0) Tris 1.21 gEDTA 0.37 gDistilled water 1 LMix to dissolve. Adjust pH to 9.0. Add 0.5 mL of Tween 20 and mix well. Store at room temperature for 3 months or at 4°C for longer storage.

gDistilled water 1 LMix to dissolve. Adjust pH to
mL of Tween 20 and mix well. Store at room temperature for 3 months or at 4°C for longer storage.

steps.instruction: PBST 990 mL PBS0.1% Tween 20 -- 10 mL (10% Tween 20) or 1mL (100% tween)
Blocking buffer 10% Normal Serum1% BSA0.25% NP40In 1x PBS

4x Blocking Buffer stock (50 ml) 0.5g BSA in 40 ml of 1xPBS. Mix to dissolve. It can be warmed in a water bath.Add 1.25 ml of 10% NP40 and mix by inverting. Do not vertexing. It causes a lot of bubbles.Add 1x PBS up to 50 ml Vol.Aliquot (250 ul in 1.5 ml tube) and store at –20 C.
Working blocking buffer 250ul of 4x blocking buffer100 ul of normal serum (if all secondary antibodies are from one species)650 ul of 1x PBS
Sodium Citrate, pH 6.0 2.94g Tri-Sodium Citrate800 mL distilled (pure) WaterMix, dissolveAdjust pH to 6.0 with 1M HCITop off to 200 mL using Pure diH2OAdd 0.5 mL Tween 20, or 5mL 10% Tween

TBST 50ml of 20x TBS 950ml DI H2O 1ml tween (cut tip to ensure you get one full ml, add to bottle)
Trace edge of slide cover with clear nail polish, then return to 4 °C, DRY slide box Nail polish adds to longevity of slides by preventing dry-out.
